steps.title: Sign up for Azure free account
steps.description: Create Azure account at azure.microsoft.com/free. Note down your Subscription ID from the Azure Portal under Subscriptions.

steps.title: Install tools locally
steps.description: Install Azure CLI and Terraform on your machine. Verify with az --version and terraform --version.

steps.title: Create a Service Principal
steps.description: Run az ad sp create-for-rbac to create a robot deployer account. Save the appId, password, and tenant values — you only see the secret once.

steps.title: Create Terraform state storage
steps.description: Manually create an Azure resource group, storage account, and container to hold Terraform state. Three az CLI commands — see implementation-steps.md.

steps.title: Write the Terraform files
steps.description: Create the infra/ folder and write main.tf, variables.tf, resource_group.tf, app_service.tf, mysql.tf, static_web_app.tf, outputs.tf.

steps.title: Run Terraform
steps.description: Run terraform init, terraform plan, then terraform apply to provision all Azure resources. Write down all output values.

steps.title: Add GitHub Actions Secrets
steps.description: Add all secrets to GitHub repo Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions. Values come from Step 3 (credentials), Step 1 (subscription ID), and Step 6 (Terraform outputs).

steps.title: Update backend code
steps.description: Add context.Database.Migrate() to Program.cs for automatic migrations on startup. Create appsettings.Production.json as a template file.

steps.title: Write backend deploy pipeline
steps.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-backend.yml — runs dotnet test, dotnet publish, then zip deploys to Azure App Service on push to main.

steps.title: Write frontend deploy pipeline
steps.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-frontend.yml — runs npm build then deploys dist/ to Azure Static Web Apps on push to main.

steps.title: Check existing CI workflows
steps.description: Review backend-ci.yml and frontend-ci.yml. These run on PRs and act as quality gates. No changes needed unless you want to tighten them.

steps.title: First deploy
steps.description: Commit and push everything to main. Watch the Actions tab in GitHub — both cd-backend and cd-frontend pipelines should run and go green.

steps.title: Verify end to end
steps.description: Check the backend Swagger UI loads at the App Service URL. Open the frontend Static Web App URL. Try registering and logging in to confirm the database migrations ran.
